questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Using computer techniques, researchers analyse layers of paint buried beneath the surface layers of old paintings. They claim that, in this way, the layered structure of a mountainous scenery once appeared in Leonardo da Vinci's paintings, was later painted over. Skeptics reply to these claims. However, that X-ray examinations of the Mona Lisa reveal no such mountains. Which of the following, if true, would tend most to weaken the force of the skeptics' objection?

choices.a: There is no written or anecdotal record that Leonardo da Vinci painted over major areas of his Mona Lisa.
choices.b: Painters of the Vinci's time commonly created images of mountainous scenery in the backgrounds of portraits like the Mona Lisa.
choices.c: No one knows for certain what parts of the Mona Lisa may have been painted by da Vinci's assistants rather than by da Vinci himself.
choices.d: Infrared photography of the Mona Lisa has revealed no trace of hidden mountain scenery.
choices.e: Analysing relying on X-rays only has the capacity to detect hidden pigments in layers of paint beneath a painting's surface.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: While Governor Verdant has been in office, the state's budget has been balanced every year. While the previous governor was in office, the state's budget increased by an average of 11.5 per cent each year. Obviously, the austere budgets during Governor Verdant's terms have caused the slowdown in the growth in state spending. Which of the following, if true, would most seriously weaken the conclusion drawn above?

choices.a: The rate of inflation in the state averaged 10 per cent each year during the previous governor's term in office and 3 per cent each year during Verdant's term.
choices.b: Both federal and state income tax rates have been lowered considerably during Verdant's term in office.
choices.c: In each year of Verdant's term in office, the state's budget has shown some increase in spending over the previous year.
choices.d: During Verdant's term in office, the state has either discontinued or begun to charge private citizens for numerous services that the state offered free to citizens during the previous governor's term.
choices.e: During the previous governor's term in office, the state received several so called "austerity" budgets intended to reduce the gap in state spending.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Psychological research indicates that college hockey and football players are more quickly moved to hostility and aggression than are college athletes not in contact sports such as swimming. But the researchers' conclusion — that contact sports encourage and teach participants to be hostile and aggressive — is untenable. The football and hockey players were probably more hostile and aggressive to start with than the swimmers. Which of the following, if true, would most strengthen the conclusion drawn by the players?

choices.a: The football and hockey players became more hostile and aggressive during the season and remained so during the off-season, whereas there was no increase in aggressiveness among the swimmers.
choices.b: The football and hockey players, but not the swimmers, were aware at the start of the experiment that they were being tested for aggressiveness.
choices.c: The same psychological research indicated that the football and hockey players had a great respect for cooperation and team play, whereas the swimmers were more concerned with excelling as individual competitors.
choices.d: The research studies were designed to include no college athletes who participated in both contact and noncontact sports.
choices.e: Throughout the United States, more incidents of fan violence occur at baseball games than at hockey or football games.
